Ad Text:
KeyMoves is partnering with a global mid-tier accounting practice for an experienced and commercially astute professional to join their Business Advisory division as a Continuous Improvement Lead.

This is a pivotal internal role focused on enhancing operational performance, strengthening quality processes and driving improved commercial outcomes across the division. You will work closely with Business Advisory Directors to ensure the teams are well supported, appropriately resourced and positioned to deliver high-quality client service.

The Role

Working closely with the Director & COO, you will take ownership of operational efficiency across the Business Advisory division, while supporting broader firm initiatives where required.

Your key focus areas will include:

Operational & Process Improvement

Lead system, workflow and technology improvement initiatives

Review and implement standardised processes and SOPs

Support the adoption of new technologies, automation and AI tools

Drive consistent and scalable ways of working across teams

Workflow & Resource Management

Support workforce planning and cross-team resourcing

Monitor lodgement performance and ensure compliance standards are met

Provide reporting and insights to support divisional performance

Quality & Risk

Ensure processes align with legislative and professional standards

Maintain consistent review and sign-off procedures

Oversee ongoing improvement of templates and documentation

Commercial Oversight

Partner with Directors and Finance to manage WIP and billing

Identify opportunities to reduce write-offs and improve recovery

Analyse performance data to enhance productivity and profitability

About You

To be successful in this role, you will demonstrate:

8+ years’ experience within a professional services environment (Business Advisory essential)

CA or CPA qualification

Strong understanding of tax and accounting standards

Experience leading tech implementations and process improvements

Highly developed commercial acumen and analytical skills

What We Offer

A genuine leadership opportunity within a growing division

Exposure to senior decision-makers

The ability to shape operational strategy and performance

A collaborative and professional mid-tier environment

If you are looking to step into a strategic operational leadership role within a reputable and progressive firm, please feel free to reach out to Michael Phillips on 0404 168 365 or mp@key-moves.com.au

We are seeking a highly skilled Data Analyst to support a major transformation initiative involving the migration of legacy Mainframe systems to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). This role will work closely with data engineers, database administrators and application developers to translate legacy Mainframe data structures into modern OCI-compatible models, ensuring shared understanding of data lineage, constraints, and transformation logic.

Key Responsibilities:

Data Discovery & Analysis

Analyse existing Mainframe datasets, file structures, schemas, and data flows to establish a clear understanding of current-state data assets.
Document data dictionaries, lineage, and dependencies to support migration planning.
Identify data quality issues, anomalies, and integrity risks within legacy systems.
Data Mapping & Migration Support

Develop detailed source-to-target data mappings from Mainframe data sources to OCI data models.
Partner with developers to define data transformation rules, cleansing logic, and migration strategies.
Support the design of ETL/ELT pipelines for migrating data into OCI (e.g., Object Storage, Autonomous Database, Data Lakehouse).
Testing & Validation

Build and execute data validation test cases, including reconciliation of migrated data between Mainframe and OCI.
Monitor data loads, analyse variances, and work with teams to resolve discrepancies.
Support UAT and production migration cycles, ensuring data accuracy and completeness.
Required Skills & Experience:

Strong experience as a Data Analyst, ideally within migration, integration, or modernisation projects.
Familiarity with Mainframe data environments (e.g., VSAM, DB2, JCL file structures).
Hands-on experience with SQL, PL/SQL and analytical tools for data profiling and validation.
Experience with ETL/ELT processes, data modelling, or cloud-based data pipelines.
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with excellent attention to detail.
Ability to work effectively across technical and non-technical teams.
Nice-to-Have Skills

Knowledge of OCI services, particularly data-related components (e.g., Autonomous Database, Object Storage, Data Integration)
Experience in large-scale migration or cloud modernisation programs.
Understanding of data governance frameworks (e.g., lineage, cataloguing, privacy).
Familiarity with tools such as ODI, Informatica, DataStage, or GoldenGate.
Knowledge of Agile delivery methodologies.
In alignment with our commitment to fostering a flexible and dynamic work environment, we have adopted a hybrid working approach that acknowledges the importance of in-person collaboration while recognising that the determination of your work location will be based on a range of factors to ensure both individual preferences and operational requirements are met.

If you would like to be part of a culture that drives innovation, delivers results, rewards performance and encourages ideas, then please press the "Apply Now" button to submit your resume.

This isn't just another corporate strategy role. This is a unique opportunity to build a critical new function from the ground up. Our client has a new, distinct business entity on a mission to improve how people in Australia navigate their healthcare. 

They are in the "greenfield" phase—building strategy, team, and their future from scratch. We are looking for a foundational leader to be the founding member of their Commercial Strategy and Analytics function.

As the Founding Commercial Strategy Lead, you will be the 2IC to the Head of Data, tasked with creating the entire commercial data playbook, defining the 3-year roadmap, and ultimately, building a team to execute your vision.

What You’ll Do:
Build from zero: Design and implement the commercial data and analytics strategy from the ground up.
Bridge Policy and Profit: Translate high-level health strategy and policy into actionable, data-driven commercial initiatives.
Own the Roadmap: Develop the 3-year strategic vision for how we use data to drive commercial outcomes and improve customer health navigation.
Influence & Partner: Act as a key strategic partner to senior leadership, building relationships across finance, clinical, and product teams to drive alignment and secure investment.
Lead & Grow: Chart the course to build and lead a future team of analysts and data professionals.
What else is in it for you?
The Founder Opportunity: This is a rare chance to get in on the ground floor and build a critical business function in your own image.
Unmatched Impact: Your work will create a lasting legacy, directly shaping the future of a major new player in Australian healthcare.
The Best of Both Worlds: Enjoy the agility and ownership of a startup, with the stability, resources, and unlimited project budget of a major corporate backer.
A Path to Leadership: Success in this role has a clear and defined trajectory to building and leading your own significant team.
If you are a strategic, entrepreneurial leader who is ready to build, we want to hear from you.

Apply now to be a part of our foundational journey.

Role: Data Architect & Governance Lead

Contract Length: Initial contract until 18 Dec 2026

Join a critical Data Program delivering enterprise-grade data visibility and governance across critical business systems and Snowflake.

This is a senior, hands-on role reporting to the Senior Project Manager, partnering closely with the CTO, CDO, Cyber and Enterprise Architecture to embed a sustainable governance operating model.

The Role

You'll lead the design and rollout of a governed, searchable enterprise data catalogue in Collibra, enabling risk reduction, privacy compliance, and downstream data minimisation initiatives.

Experience with M365 / SharePoint governance patterns

Records management integration experience

We are seeking a contract Business / Functional Analyst for an initial 2 month contract with a view to extend. This position will be part of a major Security Access Modernisation initiative that is transitioning from legacy identity and access management systems to the future state platform.
 
This position will involve working closely with architects, developers, product owners, and business stakeholders to analyse requirements, shape solution designs, and ensure successful application migration into the AppConnect ecosystem.
 
Key Responsibilities

Lead business and functional analysis activities for application migration from legacy systems to AppConnect.
Analyse IAM related requirements, including authentication flows, onboarding processes, access governance, and integration patterns.
Work with architects and developers to define solution requirements and system behaviours aligned with IAM capabilities.
Document business requirements and functional designs using user stories, acceptance criteria, wireframes, mock‑ups, and related artefacts.
Facilitate workshops with business stakeholders, technical teams, and product owners to elicit requirements and validate solution designs.
Capture and map as‑is and to‑be business processes, identifying opportunities for process improvement and re‑engineering.
Develop or contribute to system integration specifications, including data mapping, API requirements, and identity provider/service provider interactions.
Support solution demonstrations, user onboarding activities, and User Acceptance Testing (UAT), including test scenario development and defect triage.
Assist with change‑related activities such as stakeholder engagement, training support, and communication of migration impacts.
 
To successfully deliver this outcome, you will require technical and system knowledge which include
 

Strong understanding of identity and access management (IAM) concepts, including federated authentication, SSO, user lifecycle management, and access governance.
Experience with IAM platforms such as IBM Security Verify Access, AppConnect, Azure AD, Okta, Ping, or similar technologies.
Familiarity with authentication and authorisation protocols such as SAML, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, and related integration patterns.
Understanding of system integration, APIs, data mapping, and interface specifications.
Experience working in Agile delivery environments using tools such as Jira and Confluence.
 
Key Selection Criteria

Experience in system solution analysis within complex enterprise environments.
Demonstrated experience providing Agile business analysis and functional analysis services.
Extensive experience documenting requirements and functional designs using user stories, wireframes, mock‑ups, and similar artefacts.
Strong capability in negotiating with business stakeholders, users, technical teams, and product owners to achieve agreed outcomes.
Proven ability to lead and facilitate workshops with diverse stakeholder groups.
Experience in process re‑engineering, including capturing as‑is and to‑be processes using process flows.
Experience developing or contributing to system integration specifications, particularly in IAM contexts.
Experience showcasing system solutions and supporting User Acceptance Testing activities.
IAM domain experience, including authentication flows, onboarding processes, and access governance, with exposure to modern IAM platforms.

About the Role

Our client is undertaking a major business readiness project in conjunction with the Shared Service Provider to upgrade their finance system from SAP ECC6 to S/4HANA.

As the Business Analyst, you will work closely with the Project Manager, key embedded finance Subject Matter Experts, Change and Training Leads, finance stakeholders and delivery partners to support the delivery of improved, future-ready capabilities in the upgraded S/4HANA environment.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse as-is processes in SAP ECC6 and contribute to the design of future-state processes within S/4HANA, ensuring alignment with Whole-of-Government finance policies and standards.
Assist with configuration reviews, business process re-engineering, and finance data mapping
Support data migration planning, including data cleansing, validation, and reconciliation in collaboration with finance and ICT teams.
Prepare and review functional documentation including business process models, business requirement specifications, and user stories.
Participate in solution design workshops and walkthroughs, ensuring finance business needs are clearly understood and incorporated.
Develop test plans, facilitate user acceptance testing (UAT), and support defect management from a finance perspective.
Key Requirements

Demonstrated knowledge of SAP ECC6 finance modules (FI/CO) and an understanding of new capabilities introduced in S/4HANA (e.g., Universal Journal, Fiori apps).
Experience in large-scale SAP projects, particularly within finance environments.

If you live in Australia or New Zealand, you've likely heard of AMP. But at a time when society is changing, we are too. We're now a nimbler business with new leadership and thinking.

For us, these are exciting times. There's a real potential for big thinkers to help us redefine what financial services could be. And turn our legacy into something even more positive and powerful for the future.

Help people create their tomorrow, while you create yours

We help people with their banking, super, retirement and finances. Through upturns, downturns, recessions, and major life transitions. Every day, we help people see and make more of their financial potential, so that they can create their tomorrow. And we've been doing it for over 170 years.

If we do our job well, we genuinely add to the prosperity of our country and its people.

How you'll make an impact

The Lead Business Analyst brings deep subject matter expertise in Wealth Management and Superannuation and is responsible for consulting with business and technology leaders to create their strategy and associated delivery roadmaps.

As Lead Business Analyst, reporting into the Delivery Manager, you will be responsible for creating and defining innovative solutions and leading the work of for team of Business Analysts.

You'll have a comprehensive understanding of the Business Analysis practices, own and drive the analysis process for the North Platform and form a business analysis community centred around continuous improvement.

As a leader in creating solutions to meet business problems, you'll be a key person for value streams their business executives and leadership teams.

What you'll bring to the role


Business partnerships and strategy development. Consult with technology and business leaders to create solutions to drive the delivery of their strategy.
Responsible for leading and directing analysis activities for an initiative, including leading the work of Business Analysts within a delivery squad.
Custodian of the Business Analysis practice, processes and tools, forming a community of practice to oversee and improve the way Business Analysis is performed.
Govern and oversee a delivery squad's analysis, documentation and processes follow set out standards, are of the highest quality (balancing efficiency) and maintain consistency over time and across the team.
Drive continuous improvement in the delivery team's analysis output and provides constructive feedback and coaching to the team that fosters ongoing improvement.
Lead delivery risk assessment and is responsible for identifying, raising, reporting and mitigating risks involved in and discovered through analysis activities.
Lead the adoption of business agility, and delivery within an Agile framework, facilitating ceremonies (e.g. standups, retros) ensuring progress and continuous improvement.
Part of the values stream leadership team working with Delivery Managers, Project Managers and Tech Leads in analysis planning, tracking and reporting activity from project and squad through to portfolio level.
Leads the business in forming scope and initial estimates for new development ideas and proposals, contributing deep knowledge and coordinating technical experts where needed.
Actively engages in analysis while also coordinating analysis work across the Business Analysis team to navigate complex challenges and enable efficient and effective delivery. Supports hiring and responsible for onboarding Business Analysts through mentoring and 'buddying'.


You'll thrive here if…

If you can adapt from BAU to the ambiguous with ease, you'll do well here. Change is never easy, so bring your commitment, grit and growth mindset.

Because we run lean, you'll be expected to jump in and deliver across a variety of areas. Meaning, you'll be closer to the action and executive decisions that influence where we go next.

If you're someone that can hold their own, you'll find AMP quite liberating.

Why we think you'll love working at AMP

Doing what we've always done is not an option, so your clever ideas will get airtime here. You'll be encouraged to speak up and try new things. If they don't work, we move on - better for it.

We know there's no one way of doing things. So, you won't have to sacrifice who you are or how you work to fit in here. We're inclusive and flexible in many of the ways you'd expect. And in some of the ways you wouldn't. As long as your health and wellbeing come first - at home and at work.

In fact, most of what makes AMP such a welcoming, enjoyable place to work are our people. Wherever you go, you'll find moments to connect, feel valued and do meaningful work.

Whether it's through our first-class leaders who are invested in you and your success. Through year-round opportunities to volunteer, fundraise and give back to the community. Or in the everyday challenges you face as we work together to strengthen this great organisation. Challenges that will stretch you, amplify your potential and compound the impact you have.

We believe in the power of inclusion and diversity

We're dedicated to fostering inclusion, diversity, and a warm feeling of belonging at AMP. It sparks creativity, ignites innovation, and turns up the dial on the quality of our decisions and performance. This not only makes our workplace more engaged, but also leads to better connections with our customers.

We're your allies in the search for the perfect fit - when you apply, let us know how we can support you to put your best self forward during our selection process.

We're also committed to enhancing employment opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, so we enthusiastically encourage candidates from these backgrounds to apply and explore our Reconciliation Plan on our website

Ready to create your tomorrow?

If you're someone who sees opportunity where others see challenge, come and work with us in smart, progressive ways as we transform an iconic Australian brand for the future. And, through a series of career-defining moments, create your own tomorrow.

Don't procrastinate! We review applications when we get them, and if we discover the ideal candidate, we may close the role earlier than the advertised close date.

Looking forward to meeting you.

At our core, RSL LifeCare provides care and services to veterans and seniors who live in our communities or are receiving support at home. Our vision of creating proud communities living their best lives is at the heart of everything we do as we continue to build upon our legacy of supporting veterans and their families for more than 100 years.

This is an opportunity for you to join a large social purpose organisation where your skills and compassion will be valued and enable you to make a meaningful difference to people's lives.

We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to play a key role in shaping and optimising the way RSL LifeCare operates. You will work closely with stakeholders across the organisation to document current and future business processes, analyse system and process gaps, and translate insights into practical, effective solutions.

Your work will directly influence how our teams deliver services, use systems, and achieve better outcomes for the people we support.

What you will be doing:

Map out how our processes work today and how they should work in the future, creating clear and practical documentation everyone can use.
Understand how our systems connect, identify what's working and what's missing, and use gap analysis to guide smarter, more effective solutions.
Turn insights into real improvements across processes, systems, and training, making sure changes genuinely support our people and meet agreed goals.
Bring teams together through workshops, conversations, and clear requirements gathering so everyone is aligned and confident about what's needed.
Work closely with business teams, partners, and vendors to prioritise what matters most and deliver changes that make everyday work easier and more efficient.

You will have:

Significant experience in systems implementation.
Demonstrated ability to capture and document business processes.
Experience facilitating requirements workshops and translating insights into solutions.
Strong gap analysis and system evaluation skills.
High attention to detail and a continuous improvement mindset.
Ability to work effectively in ambiguous or evolving environments.
Proficiency with Office365 and SharePoint.
Qualifications in project management and/or process methodology.

RSL Life Care offers the following benefits:

Not-for-profit tax benefits - salary packaging of up to $15,900 per year. This allows you to reduce your taxable income by receiving part of your salary as tax-free benefits like super contributions, car leasing, and more.
Staff discounts across 700 select retail stores through Maxxia Rewards
Fitness Passport
Leave Loading - 17.5% pay top up on paid leave

Our commitment to diversity:

We are proud to stand for our unique values: Do the Right Thing, Aim Higher, Care from the Heart, and Act as One team.

We welcome and encourage applications from people across our diverse community, especially from Veterans, current serving ADF personnel, their families and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander applicants. We believe everyone has the right to feel safe, respected, valued and heard regardless of their gender, sexual orientation, ethnicity or disability.


It is a mandatory requirement for a team member working in the Aged Care sector to have a valid Police Check or a valid National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) check, or potentially both, to undertake the inherent requirements of the role. Clearances must be verified prior to commencing employment with RSL LifeCare.

Job Title: Head of Continuous Improvement and Efficiencies
Ad Text:
Air Liquide Australia is a market leader Gases, Technologies and Services for Industry in Australia. Air Liquide Australia provides innovative, responsive and customer focused services to our product range, which improves the performance of our customers while helping protect the environment.

Reporting to the Pacific Managing Director, we are looking for a Head of Continuous Improvement and Efficiencies to lead our organisation-wide transformation. It’s a strategic leadership position where you will challenge the status quo, coach senior business leaders, and turn innovative ideas into high-impact financial reality. Based in our Melbourne Head Office, you will be the driving force behind our efficiency roadmap across the Pacific.




How will you CONTRIBUTE and GROW?

Your primary objective is to lead the Pacific special projects and the broader Continuous Improvement (CIMP) program. You will be the central hub for operational brilliance, focusing on:

Own and drive the entity cost control and efficiency programme: Through large and small projects you will be responsible for working alongside our business leaders helping them identify, develop and execute efficiency programmes.   

Special Projects: Take full ownership of end-to-end projects aimed at controlling and sustaining the P&L. You will engage with SBL Heads to conduct assessments, provide high-level recommendations, and execute efficiency initiatives that move the needle.

CIMP Program Leadership: Drive a culture of "bottom-up" efficiency. As a qualified CIMP practitioner, you will coach teams to identify lean opportunities, develop programmes of work and ensure successful execution. The role involves leading the CIMP belt programme, where you will facilitate employees in achieving their CIMP qualifications and successfully completing their individual projects.

Capital Process Ownership: Act as the business process owner for the entity capital budget. You will forecast annual needs, manage quarterly allocations, and guide the Investment Committees on high-stakes project submissions.

Steering Committee Influence: Serve as an active member of the IM Leadership team, providing data-driven advice and challenging the direction of the business to ensure sustainable growth. You will own the IM KPI programme ensuring relevant data is captured and presented to the IM Leadership team. 

Local FIT Champion: In this capacity as the local FIT PMO, your key responsibilities will involve leading the implementation of FIT value initiatives and fostering the FIT Performance Culture.




Are you a MATCH?

Analytical Powerhouse: Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, or Engineering. You possess the financial literacy to manage a P&L and the technical mindset to understand complex supply chain and manufacturing operations.

Proven Project Leader: You possess a strong foundation in end-to-end project management, paired with the deep understanding of business operations that comes directly from first-hand operational experience.

Lean Expert: You have a strong background in LEAN principles and ideally hold a CIMP Green Belt (or have the drive to achieve one on the job).

Influencing Leader: You excel at coaching leaders, facilitating change, and communicating with impact across all levels of the organization.

Strategic Decision Maker: You can navigate various solution pathways, using quantitative reasoning to provide "Go or No Go" recommendations for major investments.




We OFFER

We provide a high-performance environment where your professional evolution is our priority:

Benefits and Support: Salary plus a performance bonus, 14 weeks paid parental leave, Salary Continuance Insurance, free on-site parking and Stock Purchase Plan options.

Work-Life Balance: Full-time permanent role with Hybrid work arrangements and flexible working hours

Professional Development: Formal CIMP certification pathways and specialized project management training with direct visibility to the Sr Management Team across regions

Wellbeing & Culture: Access to health initiatives, employee assistance programs, social groups, and retail discounts with over 350 partners.




About Air Liquide

A world leader in gases, technologies and services for Industry and Health, Air Liquide is present in 78 countries with approximately 64,500 employees and serves more than 3.8 million customers and patients. Oxygen, nitrogen and hydrogen are essential small molecules for life, matter and energy. They embody Air Liquide’s scientific territory and have been at the core of the company’s activities since its creation in 1902.




Our Differences make our Performance
At Air Liquide, we are committed to build a diverse and inclusive workplace that embraces the diversity of our employees, our customers, patients, community stakeholders and cultures across the world.

We welcome and consider applications from all qualified applicants, regardless of their background. We strongly believe a diverse organization opens up opportunities for people to express their talent, both individually and collectively and it helps foster our ability to innovate by living our fundamentals, acting for our success and creating an engaging environment in a changing world.

Who We Are

 

iSelect and Compare the Market are trusted, well-known brands dedicated to helping millions of Australians compare and buy personal finance and household products such as insurance, energy, and loans. Together, we provide a seamless, customer-focused experience that makes it easier for Australians to find the right products to suit their needs.

 

 

What You’ll Be Doing

Design and implement data pipelines to extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from ERP (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, Technology One), HRIS (e.g., Workday, UKG), payroll systems (e.g., Employment Hero, MicrOpay, Payroll Metrics), and enterprise automation platforms.
Develop and maintain data integrations with external financial systems including banking platforms (e.g., payment gateways, direct debit services, reconciliation tools) and taxation systems (e.g., ATO, GST reporting, Single Touch Payroll, PAYG).
Build and manage data flows from recruitment platforms such as LinkedIn, SEEK, and other talent acquisition systems.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver actionable insights through dashboards, reports, and analytics.
Ensure data quality, integrity, and compliance across all integrated systems.
Monitor and optimize data workflows for performance and reliability.
Maintain comprehensive documentation for data architecture, mappings, and technical specifications.
Work closely with cloud and architecture teams to deploy and manage data solutions in Databricks and Power BI.

 

 

What’s In It For You

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect, we’re all about helping people make smart choices – and joining us might just be yours.  We offer an environment where your ideas are heard, your contributions are valued, and your growth genuinely matters. Collaboration is part of our DNA, and you’ll be joining a supportive, forward-thinking team where you can do meaningful work (and be recognised for it!) 

 

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

 

Plus, we offer a range of great perks to help you thrive both in and out of work:

Vibrant and social community who have annual celebrations, family fun days and regular events
Enjoy flexible work arrangements, including the option of one day working from home each week or a 9-day fortnight
Enjoy additional leave days - ‘ME’ leave and ‘Volunteer Day’ leave
Option to purchase additional leave
Return To Work bonus for returning parents
Quarterly Award and Recognition program 

 

What Sets You Apart

Strong experience in data engineering and analytics, including ETL processes and data modelling.
Proven track record working with ERP, HR, payroll, finance, and recruitment data.
Familiarity with banking data structures, payment processing, and financial reconciliation.
Experience with taxation data and compliance reporting (e.g., Single Touch Payroll, BAS, PAYG).
Hands-on experience with payroll data and understanding of regulatory requirements.
Proficiency in SQL and programming languages such as Python, R, or Scala.
Experience with AWS data services including Glue and EventBridge.
Experience with data platforms like Snowflake or Databricks.
Understanding of secure data handling protocols and privacy standards.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect we’re proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and we embrace diversity.  

 

This role is available to candidates with unlimited Australian working rights only. Sponsorship is not available.  

 

Compare the Market and iSelect are committed to protecting your privacy. All applications and personal information are kept confidential and securely stored in line with Australian privacy laws, used only for recruitment purposes, and not shared with third parties outside the Auto and General Holdings Group without your consent.

There’s never been a more exciting time to be in energy and no better place to build your career than at Endeavour Energy.

Every day, more than 2.7 million people across New South Wales rely on us to deliver safe, reliable power. With over 1,700 employees, we’re one of the largest employers in Greater Western Sydney and a key driver of Australia’s energy transition.

You don’t need prior energy experience to succeed in this role. We’re looking for talented, curious professionals who are ready to take on challenges, grow their skills, and contribute to a culture built on care, collaboration, and belonging. Together, we put customers first and lead the way toward a smarter, cleaner, more sustainable energy future.

About the Role

Reporting to the Manager Economic Strategy, the Regulatory Modelling Analyst develops, maintains, and interrogates economic and financial models and large regulatory datasets that underpin Endeavour Energy’s submissions to the AER and IPART. This role requires advanced analytical expertise, strong technical skills, and the ability to translate complex modelling outputs into clear, actionable insights for internal stakeholders. Your work will guide regulatory, investment, and strategic decisions that drive material business outcomes.

Key Responsibilities
Develop, maintain, and document regulatory economic and financial models, including building block outcomes, incentive mechanisms, and benchmarking analysis
Review regulatory datasets submitted to the AER and IPART to identify opportunities to improve outcomes
Analyse regulatory determinations to assess impacts on revenue, expenditure forecasts, incentives, and risk profiles
Conduct sensitivity, scenario, and stress testing to inform regulatory strategy and executive decision-making
Provide robust quantitative analysis to support regulatory submissions and responses to information requests
Translate complex modelling results into clear, decision-ready insights for internal stakeholders
Monitor regulatory developments, guideline changes, and emerging methodologies to ensure compliance and improve modelling capability

Qualifications

Tertiary qualification in Economics, Finance, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Data Analytics, or a related quantitative discipline

Experience & Skills

Demonstrated experience applying quantitative analysis, economic or financial modelling to support complex business or regulatory decisions
Strong analytical and data capability, including experience managing large, complex datasets with integrity, transparency, and reproducibility
Advanced proficiency with analytical and modelling tools (e.g., Excel-based models, statistical or data analysis software)
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, able to explain technical analysis clearly to non-technical stakeholders
Practical understanding of economic or regulatory frameworks and the ability to interpret externally defined rules, guidelines, and determinations
Proven ability to deliver high-quality analytical outputs within governance frameworks and tight deadlines
Experience in the electricity industry or other regulated environments (utilities, infrastructure, government, economic regulation, or financial services), ideally supporting regulatory resets

For further information, please contact recruitment@endeavourenergy.com.au

Applications close: 19 March 2026

When we power together, we can achieve so much more.

At Endeavour Energy, diversity and inclusion make us stronger, safer, and better. We strive to create a workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and supported to thrive.

As a critical infrastructure organisation, background checks will be conducted to ensure the security and protection of our assets.

We are AUSIEX
  
With over 25 years of experience in the local market and the backing of a multinational trading technology powerhouse, we’re AUSIEX. We specialise in equities execution, clearing & settlement services, and equities administration for financial intermediaries. Since 1994, we’ve used our deep, local expertise to deliver solutions that seamlessly connect our clients to markets. We are powered by Nomura Research Institute (NRI), a global leader in technology and operations services. We’re infusing NRI’s proven technology capability and tradition of innovation, with our trusted trading solutions and local market expertise.
  
In 2025, we expanded our group by welcoming FIIG Securities, a respected Australian fixed income specialist with over 25 years of experience. FIIG Securities manages billions under advice and serves 6,000 private clients, providing investors with access to expert insights and investment opportunities across the fixed income asset class. Their focus on delivering consistent, reliable income and capital stability perfectly complements our service offering.

See yourself in our team
  
FIIG has a varied technology stack, which helps us scale our business within both the front and back office. Between these systems, there are a number of complex business processes & integrations which need to be monitored, managed, and improved to ensure no outages or core business issues.

The role of the Technical Business Analyst at FIIG will be responsible for:

Working with the business to capture, validate, prioritise and document the business requirements that will be implemented by the solutions
Working with the business and IT teams to develop designs that meet the requirements, and can be suitably implemented within the chosen technology
Working with the business and IT teams to develop business cases to help prioritise and focus spending / effort to be in line with immediate and strategic needs
The key to this role will be understanding not only the technology that supports the business, but the business processes themselves in order to successfully deliver feature enhancements and improvements.

What will you be doing  

You will be expected to perform the following tasks and responsibilities in a manner consistent with AUSIEX’s Values of Listen, learn, grow, Lead with passion take the initiative and Collaborate to innovate.
  
FIIG will be progressively rolling out a full client-facing business platform replacement. The objective of this replacement is to support client acquisition and revenue growth, business optimisation, and leverage AI technology to achieve a high standard of customer service and efficiency across FIIG’s entire business. This staged replacement must be designed and implemented in a way that ensures minimal impact to clients and normal business operations.

This role is based in Brisbane and will be accountable for the following key responsibilities of the Technical Business Analyst supporting AUSIEX’s Fixed Income Division (FIIG):

To work with the business to capture, validate, prioritise and document the business requirements that will be implemented by the solutions
To work with the business and IT teams to develop designs that meet requirements (functional, non-functional and technical), and that can be suitably implemented within the chosen technology
To work with the business and IT teams to develop business cases to help prioritise and focus spending / effort to be in line with immediate and strategic needs
Performing investigative analysis on existing systems to baseline current state requirements and design (for both the business and technical domains);
Perform the key issue analysis needed as inputs into meetings with the business and IT teams so that decisions can be made effectively. Ensure the actions and outcomes of these meetings are recorded and any outstanding actions are raised with management
Assist with the development of suitable methodologies, standards and principles
Ensuring that documentation is produced in a timely manner, is in line with standards, and that requirements are traceable to designs (and vice-versa)
Identify potential areas of process improvement (in the business and in the project), and raise these for discussion
Ensure that designs are documented to the level required, and appropriately reviewed / validated to minimise the likelihood of design flaws being found in development
Structure and facilitate meetings for project scoping, requirement collection and Current State / Future State documentation
Establish and maintain effective working relationships with all levels of the organisation
Provide accurate estimates for assigned work to project managers
We’d love to hear from passionate people who have: 
  
Essential skills

3+ continuous years’ experience as a Technical Business Analyst (or equivalent position);
Must have solid experience in performing investigative business analysis on existing systems to baseline current state requirements and design;
Strong experience developing designs based on (and integrating to) COTS (Commercial-Of-The-Shelf) products;
Extensive knowledge of technology and business operations for more than one business sector;
Proven experience working collaboratively with multiple partners to deliver successful outcomes for complex business / system problems;
Qualifications

Relevant tertiary qualification
Previous experience in: Copilot Pro, Power Apps, JIRA, Confluence, Microsoft Dynamics CRM 365, Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio
Previous experience within financial services is desirable
Previous experience with SimCorp Dimension is desirable
Behavioural Competencies

Able to demonstrate the ability to learn new systems, environments and business processes rapidly
Problem solving, ability to break problems down and step through components to replicate issues
Ability to meet tight deadlines and to react positively to shifting priorities
Communication with stakeholders including senior business leaders and external vendors
Ability to work independently and as part of a team
Ability to identify and raise issues early
Committed work ethic and accountability
Establish and maintain effective working relationships with all levels of the organisation 
  
We want to help grow your skills and develop your career with us! If this sounds like you – apply today!
  
Inclusion and diversity are part of who we are. Together, we continue to build an inclusive culture that enables, values and celebrates the diverse opinions of our employees.
  
If you need assistance or adjustments to fully participate in the application/interview process, please contact careers@ausiex.com.au.
  
Visit us on www.ausiex.com.au or on LinkedIn

We are seeking a Senior Business / IT Demand Analyst to lead the evaluation, shaping, and definition of new IT demand across Applications, Infrastructure, SaaS, and Corporate IT services. This role is a short term contract with a view to extend, based in Perth onsite. Ensuring that business needs are clearly understood, strategically aligned, financially assessed, and sufficiently defined before progressing into design and delivery.


You will work closely with business stakeholders, architects, finance, security, and delivery teams to ensure every request is viable, valuable, and aligned to enterprise standards.

Key Responsibilities
1. Demand & Requirements Management
•     Lead structured interviews and workshops to uncover true business needs.
•     Translate business problems into clear, high‑level functional and non‑functional requirements.
•     Challenge assumptions, validate justification, and distinguish between needs and wants.
•     Produce high‑quality scope documentation including in‑scope, out‑of‑scope, and on‑the‑frame items.
•     Define assumptions, constraints, dependencies, and measurable success criteria.
2. Business & Financial Analysis
•     Conduct preliminary Business Impact Assessments (BIA) covering operational, compliance, security, and continuity considerations.
•     Identify risks and assess business impact.
•     Develop high‑level cost estimates (CAPEX/OPEX) and identify licensing, infrastructure, integration, implementation, and run‑cost implications.
3. Technical & Architectural Awareness
•     Demonstrate strong understanding of enterprise applications, infrastructure, cloud, and SaaS models.
•     Understand integration patterns, identity, data flows, and security considerations.
•     Produce high‑level conceptual designs for Design Authority review.
•     Identify architectural, security, and compliance implications early in the demand lifecycle.
4. Governance & Documentation
•     Produce structured, high‑quality artefacts including scope documents, requirements packs, stakeholder analysis, risk and options analysis, and Design Authority briefings.
•     Map stakeholders by influence and impact.
•     Present clear solution options with associated risks, costs, and benefits.
5. Stakeholder & Communication Leadership
•     Engage confidently with senior stakeholders, technical teams, and delivery partners.
•     Translate technical complexity into clear, business‑friendly language.
•     Influence decisions diplomatically and manage competing priorities.
•     Build strong relationships across business and IT functions.
6. Commercial & Delivery Awareness
•     Understand procurement processes, vendor evaluation, and SaaS licensing models.
•     Assess vendor risks, contractual considerations, SLAs, and data protection requirements.
•     Support smooth transition from demand shaping into project delivery.
7. Project Delivery Support
•     Where required, perform core Business Analyst activities during the delivery lifecycle, ensuring continuity from demand through to implementation.

About You
You will thrive in this role if you:
•     Have extensive experience in business analysis, demand management, or IT portfolio intake.
•     Are comfortable operating at the intersection of business strategy, technology, and governance.
•     Can challenge constructively, think strategically, and communicate with clarity.
•     Bring strong analytical, documentation, and stakeholder‑management skills.
•     Understand enterprise‑scale IT environments and can translate complexity into actionable insight.

Why Join Us?
•     Influence strategic technology decisions at an enterprise level.
•     Work across diverse business domains and technology landscapes.
•     Shape the front‑door process for IT and drive meaningful business outcomes.
•     Join a collaborative, forward‑thinking team committed to quality and continuous improvement.

Company: Office of the Inspector-General of Intelligence and Security
Ad Text:
The Office of the Inspector-General of Intelligence and Security (IGIS) is central to the independent oversight of the Australian intelligence and security agencies. The Inspector General is an independent statutory office holder with oversight of the activities of Australia’s intelligence and security agencies to ensure they act legally and with propriety, comply with ministerial guidelines and directives, and respect human rights. The work of the Inspector-General and his staff provide assurance to ministers, the parliament and the public that these agencies are using their powers as intended.

We are a small portfolio agency, proud of our contribution to the national interest. We recognise that our people are our greatest asset, we recruit and retain highly capable, motivated professionals with high levels of integrity and personal drive; and we invest in, and value, their development. Further, we offer a rewarding, positive, and inclusive environment, empowering staff to work flexibly in their contribution to the achievement of agency goals.

With the passage of the Strengthening Oversight of the National Intelligence Community Act 2025, the jurisdiction of the IGIS will expand in 2026 to all ten agencies within the National Intelligence Community with the inclusion of all of the Australian Criminal Intelligence Commission (ACIC); and the ‘intelligence functions’ of the Australian Transaction Report and Analysis Centre (AUSTRAC), the Australian Federal Police (AFP), and the Department of Home Affairs. The IGIS is expected to further grow in light of these additions, providing new and exciting opportunities for a motivated Technical Advisor to join the team.

The Opportunity

As the agencies use of technology to deliver intelligence outcomes invariably increases, so does the need for IGIS to maintain a strong practical and current understanding of those technologies and the implications to the legality and propriety of intelligence agency activities. 

The Technical Advisor leverages their strong technical knowledge and experience to inform and/or deliver relevant and effective oversight activities. These oversight activities provide assurance to Ministers, the Parliament and the public of the legality, propriety and consistency with human rights of the activities of intelligence agencies in IGIS’ jurisdiction.

You will maintain a strong, practical knowledge of relevant technologies, including Artificial Intelligence, and the legal and ethical frameworks around their use.

We will help you build an understanding of the legislative frameworks that underpin the agencies responsible for national security, and we will support and encourage you to develop a network of professional colleagues both in the National Intelligence Community (NIC) and across the APS to help you deliver the technical advice function effectively.

Key responsibilities of the Technical Advisor include:

Shape the focus areas based on technology driven risks to the legality, propriety or transparency of agency activities
Support and Educate employees on the technical capabilities used by agencies and their impact on legality, propriety and consistency with human rights 
Lead technology focused oversight activities on behalf of the IGIS
Provide technical advice and support to the Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer regarding technological developments and potential risks as required
Build productive, but independent, working relationships with colleagues in the agencies IGIS oversees to further the work of the Office
Contribute to accurate record keeping and documentation of review activities

To be successful in this role you will have:

An interest in analysing a technology, issue or activity to understand its implication to legality, propriety and consistency with human rights and report on findings and recommendations accordingly
Provided advice relating to technology issues, including Artificial Intelligence

technology literacy, skills and experiences in one or more relevant technology field, for example: 
Cyber Security and Offensive Cyber
Network Engineering and Communications
Software development and Testing
Advanced and big-data analytics and artificial intelligence
Hardware engineering
analytical and critical thinking skills
good written and verbal communication skills
ability to work productively and discretely within a highly sensitive workplace and classified environment
ability to work effectively in a small team environment during a period of change.

The Office is a small portfolio agency within the Attorney-General’s Department. In addition to your core duties, at times you may be asked to undertake tasks that are outside your usual span of duties but that are within the range of your capabilities. Supporting one another provides our people with exposure to functions across the Office and is an integral part of our positive workplace culture, providing opportunities for diverse work, building relationships and innovative methods of working.

Lead critical business readiness activities as the organisation transitions from SAP ECC6 to S/4HANA.

Your new company

Our client, a major government department, is embarking on a significant finance transformation project. This initiative will upgrade the current SAP ECC6 finance system to SAP S/4HANA - delivering modern, streamlined, and future‑ready finance capabilities. They are seeking an experienced Business Analyst with strong finance process and SAP knowledge to help drive business readiness and ensure a smooth transition to the upgraded platform. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in complex environments, enjoys working across diverse finance and ICT teams, and is passionate about delivering high‑quality outcomes in government settings.

Your new role


As the Business Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in supporting the S/4HANA upgrade by:

Analysing current SAP ECC6 finance processes and contributing to the design of future‑state processes in S/4HANA.

Assisting with configuration reviews, process re‑engineering, and finance data mapping.
Supporting data migration activities, including data cleansing, validation, and reconciliation with both finance and ICT teams.
Preparing high‑quality documentation such as business process models, requirements specifications, and user stories.
Participating in design workshops and walkthroughs to ensure business needs are clearly understood and incorporated.
Developing test plans, coordinating user acceptance testing, and supporting defect management from a finance perspective.
Contributing to training material development and broader change management activities.
Maintaining accurate, auditable documentation aligned with Queensland Government project governance standards.
Identifying and escalating project risks, issues, and dependencies.

What you'll need to succeed


Demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst on finance or SAP-related projects.
Experience working with SAP ECC6, S/4HANA, or similar ERP finance environments.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to document processes, requirements, and user stories in a clear and structured manner.
Experience in UAT preparation and execution.
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills, particularly with finance teams and technical delivery partners.
Knowledge of government finance standards (highly desirable).

What you need to do now

Job Title: Snr Technical and Functional Analyst D365 Finance
Ad Text:
About us:
Snackbrands Australia is part of Intersnack Group, a private German snack food company founded in 1968. Although internationally owned we are still very much a local company Creating Happy Snacking Moments for everyone!


Here at Snack Brands Australia, we proudly make some of Australia’s favourite snacks including Kettle, CC’s, Thins, Cheezels, The Natural Chip Company and Nice & Natural. Across our three sites, we make over 200 million packets of chips a year - that's a lot of enjoyment and fun we bring to snackers every day! We encourage a culture that is exciting, challenging and we strive for innovation in everything we do. Our aim is for our people to share in our success, enjoy our products and reap the benefits of their hard work within a supportive environment where work life balance is encouraged!


About the role:


We’re seeking an experienced Snr Functional & Technical Analyst D365 - Finance to join our IT Applications team and lead the delivery of D365 Finance projects, enhancements and support for our Finance function. If you’re a strong communicator who thrives on solving complex financial system challenges, this role is your opportunity to make a real impact.

This role will be based at our head office in Norwest with regular travel to our manufacturing site at Orchard Hills


What you’ll be responsible for:
Leading the process and system changes required in Dynamics 365 F&O, to streamline workflows and meet specific business requirements.
Develop critical project artifacts, including Business Requirement Specifications (BRS), Functional Design Documents (FDD), technical requirements and process flows.
Reduce the reliance on legacy systems, manual and XLS based processes by leveraging more Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations functionality.
Conduct Dynamics F&O working sessions with stakeholders to define requirements, demonstrate system capabilities, data migration and reporting to meet business requirements.
Partner with Finance, Production, and Logistics teams to ensure seamless, optimised and compliant end-to-end data flows and reporting.
Develop, execute and lead unit, system and user testing as required to implement changes into production
Support Finance users including production problems, incidents and change requests i.e. manage and resolve open tickets in your support queue.
Follow ITIL processes and methodologies for approval and migration of changes to production.
Work with vendors to provide support and upgrade information.
Analyse, troubleshoot and resolve issues across Microsoft D365 Finance modules, collaborating with IT and business stakeholders.
Deliver Finance related D365 projects and enhancements on budget and aligned to business goals.
Work closely with Finance leaders to ensure solutions meet operational and compliance requirements.
Build strong relationships across business functions and positively influence best-practice solution design.
Ensure compliance with broader IT strategy and architecture.



What you’ll bring to the role:
Proven experience as a Senior BA – Finance, ideally within FMCG.
Excellent Dynamics 365 and business process knowledge in Finance and Operations – able to configure modules and integrate to other legacy applications.
Solution knowledge of Dynamics 365, SQL data base and integration technologies.
Ability to research and facilitate D365 application solutions to meet business requirements
Excellent knowledge of quality assurance and security related to D365 applications.
Strong stakeholder management, negotiation and communication skills.
Ability to prepare business cases, estimates and project documentation.
Strong analytical, commercial and systems-thinking capability.
Accounting and financial management education or experience an advantage.
Experience managing multiple priorities across functions with conflicting requirements.
Knowledge of high-volume transaction and billing systems is an advantage.


What can you expect in return?
A supportive working environment (with plenty of snacks on hand!) where you'll feel engaged and valued, career growth, Birthday Leave, Access to discounts on Retailers, onsite parking (including electric car charging stations) and a focus on health and wellness (like Wellbeing Days!) to make your workplace more enjoyable!

We’re on the lookout for a strategic and hands-on Solution Analysis & Design Manager, who’s ready to roll up their sleeves and lead the delivery of high-quality solution analysis and project-level solution architecture across enterprise-wide digital and technology initiatives. Operating within our Digital & Technology (D&T) Transformation function, this role is responsible for ensuring solution designs are aligned with Enterprise Architecture principles, governance standards and UnitingCare’s 2030 Strategy.

This role will lead and guide project and program analysis and design resources (including Solution Architects, Business Analysts, technical leads and vendors), ensuring design standards, guardrails and assurance processes are consistently applied to deliver sustainable, customer-centric technology outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Own the delivery of project-level solution architecture and detailed design execution for approved initiatives, ensuring solution quality, technical coherence and alignment with delivery plans.

Ensure all solution designs are reviewed through established architecture governance and design assurance forums, addressing feedback and demonstrating compliance with enterprise architecture principles and standards.

Lead and guide D&T project and program analysis and design resources with respect to scope, deliverables, design artefacts, review processes and expected outcomes.

Partner with the Enterprise Architecture Manager to support demand intake, triage and early assessment activities, contributing feasibility input and delivery insights to inform investment decisions.

Develop and implement analysis and design standards, templates and guidance to ensure fit-for-purpose, customer-centric solution outcomes.

Drive continuous improvement of technical delivery methods, reference architectures, cost estimation models, policies and standards.

Maintain oversight of solution architecture capacity, demand and performance reporting.

Prepare senior-level presentations and reports to align stakeholders and drive informed decision-making.

Analyse industry trends, product lifecycles and technology roadmaps to support sustainable digital blueprint delivery.

Drive successful implementation of D&T initiatives by translating between technology and business stakeholders.

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Management or related discipline (or equivalent industry experience).

7+ years’ experience leading a solution analysis and design capability within complex, multi-vendor environments.

Extensive experience in business requirements gathering, high-level and detailed systems design, and implementable architecture patterns.

Demonstrated experience establishing and maintaining solution governance and design assurance processes.

Strong background across solution architecture, business analysis and/or software development.

Exceptional analytical, conceptual and problem-solving skills.

Proven ability to engage executive and technical stakeholders with high-quality presentations, reports and documentation.

Experience working across SaaS platforms, cloud (Azure), integration environments and multi-vendor ecosystems highly regarded.

Relevant certifications (TOGAF, ITIL, Agile or vendor certifications) desirable.

A collaborative leadership style, strong mentoring capability and commitment to developing technical talent.

Commitment to the mission and values of the not-for-profit sector.

 

Why you will love it here:

Working with us means stepping into a role that’s as broad as it is meaningful. You will gain exposure to:

Multiple industries under one roof - health, aged care, disability, community services, and more.

A wide range of workforce models, leadership styles, and organisational challenges.

Opportunities to stretch, grow, and shape your career in ways you won’t find elsewhere.

 

And the perks? We’ve got plenty:

Flexible work arrangements to support work-life balance.

Access to salary packaging, meal and entertainment benefits - up to $15,900 per year tax free as well as up to $2,650 of your pre-tax income to pay for meal and entertainment benefits. To learn more about this benefit watch the video via the following link: Salary Packaging Explained.     

A recognition and rewards platform, providing employee discounts at hundreds of retailers, including everyday expenses, holidays, health insurance, a wellbeing program and employee assistance program.

Career development opportunities to challenge yourself, grow and make a meaningful difference.

12 weeks paid parental leave and 2 weeks paid partner leave, available after 6 months service, in addition to the government scheme.

Diversity and inclusive leave, offering paid gender affirmation leave and cultural leave options (paid and unpaid) for those who may celebrate Lunar New Year, Diwali, Ramadan, NAIDOC weeks and other significant cultural events.

We’re committed to creating safe, inclusive, and welcoming environments for all—especially children, young people, and the elderly. Our services are built on trust, care, and a deep sense of responsibility.

Ready to bring your whole self to work and make a real difference? Apply now and join a team where your work truly matters.

Who We Are

 

iSelect and Compare the Market are trusted, well-known brands dedicated to helping millions of Australians compare and buy personal finance and household products such as insurance, energy, and loans. Together, we provide a seamless, customer-focused experience that makes it easier for Australians to find the right products to suit their needs.

 

 

What You’ll Be Doing 

Design and implement data pipelines to extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from ERP (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, Technology One), HRIS (e.g., Workday, UKG), payroll systems (e.g., Employment Hero, MicrOpay, Payroll Metrics), and enterprise automation platforms.
Develop and maintain data integrations with external financial systems including banking platforms (e.g., payment gateways, direct debit services, reconciliation tools) and taxation systems (e.g., ATO, GST reporting, Single Touch Payroll, PAYG).
Build and manage data flows from recruitment platforms such as LinkedIn, SEEK, and other talent acquisition systems.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver actionable insights through dashboards, reports, and analytics.
Ensure data quality, integrity, and compliance across all integrated systems.
Monitor and optimize data workflows for performance and reliability.
Maintain comprehensive documentation for data architecture, mappings, and technical specifications.
Work closely with cloud and architecture teams to deploy and manage data solutions in Databricks and Power BI.

 

What’s In It For You

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect, we’re all about helping people make smart choices – and joining us might just be yours.  We offer an environment where your ideas are heard, your contributions are valued, and your growth genuinely matters. Collaboration is part of our DNA, and you’ll be joining a supportive, forward-thinking team where you can do meaningful work (and be recognised for it!) 

 

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

 

Plus, we offer a range of great perks to help you thrive both in and out of work:

Vibrant and social community who have annual celebrations, family fun days and regular events
Enjoy flexible work arrangements, including the option of one day working from home each week or a 9-day fortnight
Enjoy additional leave days - ‘ME’ leave and ‘Volunteer Day’ leave
Option to purchase additional leave
Return To Work bonus for returning parents
Quarterly Award and Recognition program 

 

 

What Sets You Apart 

Strong experience in data engineering and analytics, including ETL processes and data modelling.
Proven track record working with ERP, HR, payroll, finance, and recruitment data.
Familiarity with banking data structures, payment processing, and financial reconciliation.
Experience with taxation data and compliance reporting (e.g., Single Touch Payroll, BAS, PAYG).
Hands-on experience with payroll data and understanding of regulatory requirements.
Proficiency in SQL and programming languages such as Python, R, or Scala.
Experience with AWS data services including Glue and EventBridge.
Experience with data platforms like Snowflake or Databricks.
Understanding of secure data handling protocols and privacy standards.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect we’re proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and we embrace diversity.  

 

This role is available to candidates with unlimited Australian working rights only. Sponsorship is not available.  

 

Compare the Market and iSelect are committed to protecting your privacy. All applications and personal information are kept confidential and securely stored in line with Australian privacy laws, used only for recruitment purposes, and not shared with third parties outside the Auto and General Holdings Group without your consent.

Job Title: Senior Tech Business Analyst - AI Transformation
Ad Text:
Senior Technical Business Analyst – AI Transformation

Drive real business outcomes using AI

We are currently delivering a strategic AI transformation program focused on generating measurable business outcomes across operations — including cost reduction, revenue growth and productivity improvement.

We are seeking a Senior Technical Business Analyst to play a key role in shaping and delivering these initiatives.

This role sits at the centre of the transformation — working directly with business leaders, architects and engineering teams to identify opportunities, define solutions and ensure initiatives deliver real, measurable value.

This is not a documentation-only BA role. We are looking for someone who can challenge thinking, quantify value and drive outcomes.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Driving AI Use Cases from Idea to Delivery

You will help identify, shape and deliver AI-driven initiatives that generate tangible business benefits.

Your work will include:

• Identifying opportunities where AI and automation can deliver business value
• Defining clear success metrics (cost savings, revenue uplift, productivity gains)
• Developing strong business cases to support investment decisions
• Translating business problems into technical solutions with architects and engineers

Business Case & Value Definition

You will build structured business cases that clearly define the value of each initiative.

This includes:

• Business value definition
• Cost-benefit modelling
• Measurement frameworks for:

Cost savings

Revenue improvement

Productivity gains
• Post-delivery validation of outcomes

Requirements & Solution Definition

You will lead the documentation and validation of requirements across initiatives.

Deliverables will include:

• Functional Requirements (Level 1–3)
• Non-Functional Requirements
• Context diagrams
• Process flows and swim lane diagrams
• User journeys
• Operational support documentation

You will work closely with solution architects and engineering teams to ensure requirements translate cleanly into delivery.

Delivery & Testing Support

• Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT)
• Support Production Verification Testing
• Validate solutions against expected business outcomes
• Ensure operational teams are equipped with support documentation

What We Are Looking For

We are looking for someone who can think commercially and operate technically.

You will likely come from consulting, enterprise transformation or large technology delivery environments.

Experience

• 5–10+ years experience as a Business Analyst / Technical BA
• Experience working on complex technology or transformation initiatives
• Strong experience building business cases and value frameworks
• Experience working closely with solution architects and engineering teams

Technical Capability

You should be comfortable translating business problems into technical requirements.

Experience with:
• AI, automation or data-driven solutions (highly desirable)
• End-to-end SDLC environments
• Process modelling and solution design support

Tools

Strong proficiency in:

• Jira
• Confluence
• Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Visio)

The Type of Person Who Succeeds in This Role

You are someone who:

• Can cut through ambiguity and structure complex problems
• Can challenge stakeholders constructively
• Is comfortable working in fast-moving delivery environments
• Can balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution
• Is able to work autonomously and drive outcomes

Location & Working Model

• Melbourne based
• Hybrid working model (remote only is not optional)




Why This Role is Interesting

You will:

• Work on real enterprise AI initiatives, not just theory
• Influence large operational and commercial decisions
• Work directly with senior business stakeholders and engineering teams
• Deliver solutions that impact thousands of customers and employees

Application Process

As part of the selection process, candidates may be asked to demonstrate examples of previous work including:

• Business cases
• Requirements documentation
• Process flows or user journeys
• UAT or delivery artefacts

If you are a Technical Business Analyst who wants to work on high-impact AI initiatives rather than routine BA work, we would like to hear from you.

Apply now via Seek.

Job Title: Finance Analyst / FP&A and M&A / Sydney CBD
Ad Text:
Finance Analyst / FP&A and M&A / Sydney CBD 

Sydney CBD location
Big 4 audit–trained professionals encouraged
Global business with genuine career progression
This is a standout opportunity for a Big 4–trained accountant to step into a commercially focused role within a large, internationally operating organisation.

Sitting within a lean global finance and corporate development function, you’ll work closely with senior finance leadership and gain exposure to high-value decision-making across forecasting, reporting, and corporate development initiatives. Responding to complex queries from global head office and external parties

Building a deep understanding of operational and financial value drivers
This is an ideal move for someone looking to transition from core reporting into broader commercial and strategic exposure.

This role suits a high-calibre accountant with strong Big 4 foundations and a desire to build a career in commercial finance and corporate development.
You’ll ideally bring:

Big 4 audit training (essential)
CA / CPA qualification (or equivalent)
Exposure to Deals, Transaction Services, Valuations, Investment Banking or Corporate Development (highly regarded)
Strong financial modelling, Excel and PowerPoint capability
Solid knowledge of IFRS and complex reporting
Sharp analytical thinking, commercial judgement and intellectual curiosity
Confidence operating in a fast-paced, high-accountability environment
If you’re ready to accelerate your career in a globally connected, commercially driven finance team, this is well worth exploring.

To register your interest, please submit your CV via the prompts.

For further information, contact Ed Kirby on 0419 615 720.
